result:
Based on my research, here is what I found. The Cubex Construct appears in both Act 1a (Overgrowth) as a regular monster and in Act 3 (Glory) paired with a Punch Construct. It is not a boss — it's a regular/hallway encounter enemy. However, I'll provide the best available strategic advice based on the search results.

Key findings:
- 
Cubex Construct appears in Act 1a - Overgrowth as a monster encounter
, and 
in Act 3 - Glory as "Punch Construct + Cubex Constructs"
.
- 
Cubex Construct has a "Charge Up" ability that grants it 2 Strength
.
- 
The "Punch construct + 2 cubex constructs" encounter is considered by some players to be among the toughest regular encounters
, though 
these are described as "very basic late-game AOE checks" that "test to see if you have a good deck flow or deck synergy"
.
- 
The strategy is to "poke with multi hits or AoE to bypass block" and "Silent poisons melt it passively," and to "burst the shell when block drops (every 3 or 4 turns)"
.
- 
Piercing Wail causes "ALL enemies lose 6(8) Strength this turn"
, which is excellent against Strength-stacking enemies.

```json
{
  "boss": "Cubex Construct",
  "character": "The Silent",
  "boss_mechanics": "The Cubex Construct is a construct-type enemy appearing in Act 1 (Overgrowth) solo and in Act 3 (Glory) paired with a Punch Construct and a second Cubex. It uses a 'Charge Up' ability that grants it 2 Strength, causing its attacks to escalate over time. It cycles between gaining Block (creating a defensive shell) and attacking, with its Block dropping every 3-4 turns. In the Act 3 multi-enemy variant, the combined damage and Strength scaling from multiple constructs creates a dangerous AoE check that punishes slow or single-target decks.",
  "strategy": "Use multi-hit attacks, AoE, or Poison to bypass the Cubex Construct's periodic Block phases, as Poison damage ticks passively through defenses. Focus on applying Poison early with Noxious Fumes or Deadly Poison and let it accumulate while you block incoming damage. Time your burst damage for the turns when the construct's Block drops (roughly every 3-4 turns). Use Piercing Wail or Malaise to strip the Strength it gains from Charge Up, neutering its scaling before it becomes dangerous. In the Act 3 multi-construct variant, prioritize AoE damage or spread Poison across all enemies to avoid being overwhelmed.",
  "priority_cards": [
    "Noxious Fumes",
    "Piercing Wail",
    "Corpse Explosion",
    "Deadly Poison",
    "Blade Dance",
    "Malaise",
    "Crippling Cloud",
    "Envenom",
    "Afterimage"
  ],
  "cards_to_avoid_playing": [
    "High-cost single-target attacks during Block-up turns",
    "Unupgraded Strikes (too low value per energy)",
    "Setup-heavy Power cards when constructs are already scaled (too slow)"
  ],
  "hp_threshold": "Act 1: 50+ HP recommended. Act 3 (Punch Construct + 2 Cubex Constructs variant): 45+ HP, as the multi-enemy fight can deal 20-30+ damage even with decent blocking if you lack AoE.",
  "potion_advice": "Use a Weak Potion or Dexterity Potion early to mitigate the first big attack turns. Save Poison Potion or Plague Potion for the Act 3 multi-construct variant to spread poison quickly. A Shackling Potion (ALL enemies lose 7 Strength this turn) is extremely high value here since it negates multiple stacks of Charge Up across all constructs. Don't hoard potions — this fight scales fast and an early potion prevents more HP loss than a late one."
}
```
